metadata.analyst_note: All eligibility based on cited primary sources. UNKNOWN where unverifiable. Research output only, not legal advice. Verify current NOFOs before applying.

sc_recognition_authority.statute: SC Code of Laws §1-31-40(A)(10); SC Code of Regulations Chapter 139
sc_recognition_authority.administering_agency: SC Commission for Community Advancement and Engagement (formerly Commission for Minority Affairs)

tribes.name: Waccamaw Indian People
tribes.recognition_source: SC Commission for Minority Affairs — first state-recognized tribe in SC

tribes.name: Edisto Natchez-Kusso Tribe of South Carolina
tribes.nonprofit_501c3: PROBABLE — tribe website states two 501(c) nonprofit arms; EIN not publicly confirmed

tribes.name: Pee Dee Indian Nation of Upper South Carolina

state_recognized_groups_not_full_tribes: Eastern Cherokee, Southern Iroquois and United Tribes of SC
Pee Dee Indian Nation of Beaver Creek
part_1_sc_state_recognized_tribes.groups_eligibility_note: These four hold state-recognized GROUP status (not full TRIBE status). Eligibility under provisions specifying state-recognized TRIBE vs. group is UNKNOWN — verify program-by-program.
part_1_sc_state_recognized_tribes.fiscal_sponsor_note: Any tribe without 501(c)(3) can use a 501(c)(3) fiscal sponsor to access open_nonprofit funding categories. Requires formal fiscal sponsorship agreement.

federal_open_to_state_recognized_or_nonp.program: ANA Social and Economic Development Strategies (SEDS)
federal_open_to_state_recognized_or_nonp.exact_eligibility_language: Incorporated state-recognized Indian tribes; Incorporated non-federally recognized tribes; Consortia of Indian tribes; Incorporated non-profit multi-purpose community-based Indian organizations.
federal_open_to_state_recognized_or_nonp.key_condition: Must be incorporated. Unincorporated tribe must apply via incorporated nonprofit arm.

federal_open_to_state_recognized_or_nonp.program: ANA Native American Language Preservation and Maintenance (P&M / Esther Martinez Immersion)
federal_open_to_state_recognized_or_nonp.exact_eligibility_language: Incorporated state-recognized Indian tribes; Incorporated non-federally recognized tribes; National or regional incorporated non-profit Native American organizations with Native American community-specific objectives.

federal_open_to_state_recognized_or_nonp.program: ANA Sustainable Employment and Economic Development Strategies (SEEDS)
federal_open_to_state_recognized_or_nonp.exact_eligibility_language: Same eligibility class as SEDS — incorporated state-recognized Indian tribes eligible under 42 U.S.C. §2991b

Non-federally recognized tribes ARE eligible to apply AS LONG AS the applicant is a nonprofit, tax-exempt 501(c)(3) organization with three years of arts programming at the time of application. Project participation CANNOT be restricted to only tribal members.
federal_open_to_state_recognized_or_nonp.key_conditions: 501c3 required; 3-year arts programming history required; cannot restrict project to tribal members only

federal_open_to_state_recognized_or_nonp.eligibility_basis: NEH General Terms and Conditions for Awards to Organizations
federal_open_to_state_recognized_or_nonp.exact_eligibility_language: NEH issues grants to private nonprofit organizations, institutions of higher education, and state, local, and federally recognized Indian tribal governments. Nonprofit tribal organizations with 501(c)(3) eligible via nonprofit pathway.
federal_open_to_state_recognized_or_nonp.key_condition: 501c3 required. Some NEH programs have federal-recognition set-asides — verify program-by-program.

federal_open_to_state_recognized_or_nonp.program: IHS Preparatory and Pre-Graduate Scholarships — INDIVIDUAL applicants only
federal_open_to_state_recognized_or_nonp.exact_eligibility_language: Recipients must be a member or descendant of a federally recognized, state-recognized OR terminated American Indian Tribe or Alaska Native village.
federal_open_to_state_recognized_or_nonp.key_condition: INDIVIDUAL tribal members apply — the tribe itself does not apply. IHS Health Professions Scholarship requires federally recognized membership ONLY.

SC_state_programs.agency: SC Commission for Community Advancement and Engagement
SC_state_programs.description: Commission requested $500,000 recurring (FY2027) for state-recognized entities. Up to 10% admin; remainder to tribes/groups.
SC_state_programs.caveat: Annual legislative appropriation — amount and rules vary year to year.

SC_state_programs.program: SC SHPO Historic Preservation State Grant Fund
SC_state_programs.agency: SC Department of Archives and History
SC_state_programs.exact_eligibility_language: Eligible applicants are nonprofit 501(c)(3) organizations, local governments, and federal or state recognized Native American tribes.

SC_state_programs.agency: SC Commission for Community Advancement and Engagement
SC_state_programs.caveat: Confirm active round directly with Commission.

part_2_funding_CAN_pursue.foundation_private_note: Most private/foundation grants open to 501(c)(3) nonprofits regardless of recognition. Tribes without 501(c)(3) should use fiscal sponsor. Key open funders: First Nations Development Institute (https://www.firstnations.org/grants/), Seventh Generation Fund (https://www.7genfund.org/), Native Arts and Cultures Foundation (https://www.nativeartsandcultures.org/grants).

part_3_funding_CANNOT_pursue.eligibility_language: 25 U.S.C. §5304(e): Indian tribe = organized group recognized as eligible for special programs by the United States because of their status as Indians — requires BIA Federal Register listing.

part_3_funding_CANNOT_pursue.category: IHS Self-Governance Compacts and Institutional Health Grants
part_3_funding_CANNOT_pursue.eligibility_language: 42 CFR §136.21-136.25: provision of health services grew out of government-to-government relationship with federally recognized Tribes.
part_3_funding_CANNOT_pursue.note: IHS individual scholarships ARE open to state-recognized tribe members — see Part 2.

part_3_funding_CANNOT_pursue.category: HUD Indian Housing Block Grant (IHBG / NAHASDA)
part_3_funding_CANNOT_pursue.eligibility_language: NAHASDA 25 U.S.C. §4101: Indian tribe = entity on BIA Federal Register list.

part_3_funding_CANNOT_pursue.category: HUD Indian Community Development Block Grant (ICDBG)
part_3_funding_CANNOT_pursue.eligibility_language: 24 CFR Part 1003: eligible applicants are Indian tribe or Alaska native village on BIA Federally Recognized Tribes list.

part_3_funding_CANNOT_pursue.category: EPA Indian Environmental General Assistance Program (GAP)
part_3_funding_CANNOT_pursue.eligibility_language: Eligible Applicants: Federally Recognized Tribes and Intertribal Consortium. 40 CFR §35.502.
IMLS: Tribe must be Federally recognized per BIA Federal Register list.
FY2026 SP-26-004: Eligibility limited to federally recognized AI/AN tribes, tribal organizations, UIOs, and consortia.

part_3_funding_CANNOT_pursue.category: All BIA formula grants ALN 15.xxx series
part_3_funding_CANNOT_pursue.eligibility_language: 25 U.S.C. §5304 requires BIA Federal Register listing.
Grants to federally recognized tribal governments and designated NAGs. State-recognized tribe MEMBERS eligible as PARTICIPANTS (20 CFR §668.300); tribe cannot be grantee.

part_3_funding_CANNOT_pursue.category: USDA Community Facilities — tribal government pathway
part_3_funding_CANNOT_pursue.eligibility_language: USDA CF Fact Sheet: Federally recognized Tribes listed as eligible. State-recognized tribes not listed as tribal governments.
part_3_funding_CANNOT_pursue.dual_pathway_note: 501(c)(3) tribal nonprofit IS eligible via nonprofit pathway. Gate on 501c3 before blocking.

part_3_funding_CANNOT_pursue.eligibility_language: EDA FAQ: State-recognized Tribes not eligible as Indian Tribes. Indian Tribe per 13 C.F.R. §300.3 = BIA Federal Register.
part_3_funding_CANNOT_pursue.dual_pathway_note: EDA Public Works/Economic Adjustment open to nonprofits separately.

part_4_machine_usable_ruleset.matcher_logic: state_only => eligible ONLY for recognition_requirement IN ['state_ok','open_nonprofit']. BLOCKED for 'federal_required'. For 'federal_required_for_tribal_pathway': check dual_pathway field — 501c3 nonprofit pathway may be separately open.

additional_condition_flags.incorporated_required: Must be legally incorporated. Unincorporated tribe must apply via nonprofit arm.
additional_condition_flags.individual_only: Award to individual tribal members, not tribal org.
additional_condition_flags.dual_pathway_check: Program has tribal pathway (federal_required) AND nonprofit pathway (open_nonprofit) — confirm which applies before gatekeeping.

categories.label: SC SHPO Historic Preservation State Grant Fund
WIOA Section 166 as GRANTEE
categories.note: State tribal MEMBERS eligible as PARTICIPANTS; tribe cannot be grantee.
